clusters.headline: South Africa: Matlala's Lawyers Head to Madlanga Commission - South African News Briefs - July 7, 2026
Explosions rock Damascus, wounding 18, as French President Macron visits Syria
clusters.summary: The French president's office said that Macron was safe and that his meeting with Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa would continue as scheduled.

clusters.headline: Syria says 18 wounded in Damascus blasts during Macron visit
clusters.summary:
Syria says 18 wounded in Damascus blasts during Macron visit

    
    

        
    
                The blasts occurred in central Damascus near the hotel where France's president spent the night, with the Elysee Palace saying his visit is ongoing.

clusters.headline: Nigeria: Govt Orders Probe of Meta, Google, X, AI Platforms Over Alleged Exploitation of Nigerian Media
clusters.summary: [This Day] President Bola Tinubu, yesterday, directed Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission (FCCPC) to investigate major global technology companies and Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) platforms over allegations of anti-competitive practices and the exploitation of content b

clusters.headline: Zimbabwe: Mnangagwa Seeks More Donations As Zimbabwe Struggles to Repatriate Citizens From South Africa
clusters.summary: [New Zimbabwe] President Emmerson Mnangagwa has appealed to the private sector and other well-wishers to provide additional financial support for Zimbabwe's ongoing repatriation programme to bring home citizens stranded in South Africa.

clusters.headline: Burkina Faso: Burkina Faso's Army Accused of Preventing Civilians From Leaving Jihadist-Besieged Towns
clusters.summary: [The New Humanitarian] Ouagadougou -- "They say we have to accept dying for the homeland."

clusters.headline: Man sexually exploited 71-year-old woman with dementia after walking her home, admits to rape
clusters.summary: Mohamad Zakir Jaafar thought he could get away with his crimes since the victim was senile and would not tell anyone.

clusters.headline: Watch: Crowds march through Tehran during funeral procession for Iran's slain Supreme Leader
clusters.summary:
Watch: Crowds march through Tehran during funeral procession for Iran's slain Supreme Leader

    
    

        
    
                Aerial footage from the funeral procession of Iran's late Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, on Monday showed hundreds of thousands of people

clusters.headline: Kenya: Police Tighten Nairobi Security With Checkpoints Ahead of Defiant Saba Saba Protests
clusters.summary: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Police will mount enhanced checkpoints across Nairobi on Tuesday in a major security operation ahead of planned Saba Saba demonstrations, even as activists insist they will proceed with the protests despite warnings that any gathering will be treated as unlawful.

clusters.headline: Tanzania: Tanzania Protesters Face Heavy Security, Fear Repeat of Deadly Election Violence
clusters.summary: [RFI] Tanzanians planning to protest Tuesday against government repression are facing a heavy police presence and warnings from authorities, which recalls the mass killings that followed last October's disputed election.

clusters.headline: China test-launches a ballistic missile in the South Pacific and raises regional concerns
clusters.summary: The launch, using a dummy warhead, took place the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defense treaty meant to counter Chinese influence in the Pacific.
